description_long: This is a military-style, twin-engine turboprop airplane with a high-wing configuration and four-bladed propellers mounted on each wing. The fuselage is streamlined and painted primarily in white with blue and black markings, including a prominent U.S. military insignia and the identifier 'X-19' on the nose and tail. The wings are straight and positioned above the midline of the fuselage, with large propellers suggesting vertical takeoff or STOL (Short Takeoff and Landing) capability. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with a clear canopy, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A white, military-style twin turboprop airplane with high-mounted straight wings, large four-bladed propellers, and a conventional tail. It features U.S. military markings and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted wings with large propellers, and visible military markings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propellers on each wing, the cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical wing layout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and wing structure.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, propeller arrangement, and the streamlined fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles; large four-bladed propellers mounted on each wing.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings, each driving a four-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely multi-seat based on fuselage size.
